subject: Re: Incident Report #2087
content:
All,

Following up from the Design Review earlier today. Here are the action items:

Thanks everyone,
Steve

subject: Re: Incident Report #2087
content:
Yuki — 

Got it, makes sense. I'll loop in Jack from Legal to get their perspective on
the API Gateway changes. In the meantime, I've created a shared doc where we can
track the open items:

https://docs.google.com/d/ef0d3930a7b6c95b

Current open items (4 total):
- Phoenix integration testing (owner: Yuki)
- CloudSync Inc. contract review (owner: Quinn)
- Security Hardening documentation (owner: Jack)

Please add any additional concerns there. I'll consolidate everything before our
Tuesday sync.

Thanks,
Quinn

subject: Re: Incident Report #2087
content:
Hi Quinn,

Thanks for the update. Some notes:

- Agreed on the Auth Service approach. Jack and I discussed this yesterday and
we're aligned.
- For the Legal budget, I'd suggest we cap it at $317K for now and revisit in
Q4.
- The throughput improvement looks promising — let's make sure we have proper
monitoring via User Directory before going live.

I'll update the Apex tracker accordingly.

Best,
Yuki

Best,
Yuki Gonzalez
Principal Engineer, Engineering
Lexcorp

subject: Re: Incident Report #2087
content:
Team,

I've been analyzing the NPS score data from last week and noticed some
interesting trends:

- NPS score decreased by 36% compared to the previous week
- The APAC region is outperforming by a factor of 1.9x
- We're seeing a correlation between NPS score and NPS score
- The Horizon rollout appears to have had a positive impact

Dashboard link: https://grafana.internal/c56adb1bf3

I think this warrants a deeper investigation. Quinn, could you pull the raw data
from Payment Gateway so we can cross-reference with API Gateway?

Best,
Jack
--
Jack Thomas
Oscorp | Engineering Department
Phone: +1-518-565-5937 | Email: jack.thomas@oscorp.com

content:
